content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 20, 1989 
Michael G. Balmages 
Chair, Orange County Democratic Party 
828 N. Broadway 
Santa Ana, CA 
92701 
Dear Mr. Balmages: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-145 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform 
Act,l/ specifically with regard to the contribution limitation 
provisions contained in Sections 85300-85307. 
Your letter correctly states my advice that committees formed 
to qualify, support or oppose recall measures, and committees 
formed to do voter registration are not subject to the 
contribution limitations as long as the committees do not make 
contributions to candidates. 
This is because Sections 85300-85307 
apply only to candidates and their controlled committees and to 
committees that make contributions to candidates. 
Please do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5662 if you 
have additional questions. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
l/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


February 28, 1989 
Ms. Carla Wardlow 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sucramcnto, Califoru.ia 95804- 081,"1 
Dear Ms. Wardlow: 
I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of February 
28, 1989. In that conversation, I asked you about contributions by in-
dividuals to two possible committees. One is the Orange County Voters 
Rights Committee which has been formed to promote voter rights including 
preventing harassment at the polls and doing voter registration. The other 
committee is The Committee to Recall Curt Pringle which has as its primary 
purpose investigating the possibility of and possibly doing a recall of a sitting 
Assemblyman. In answer to my question regarding contribution limits, you 
indicated that Proposition 73 contains no limit on the amounts individuals 
can donate to either of these committees, as long as none of the funds of 
these committees is used to support particular candidates. 
You indicated in our telephone conversation that you could 
confirm the accuracy of this information. I would greatly appreciate your 
doing that by initialling the enclosed copy of this letter on the space in-
dicated and returning it to me in the enclosed postage paid envelope. 
MGB/js 
Thanks for your courtesy and cooperation. 
~ours, 
Michael G. Balmages 
Chair, Orange County 
Democratic Party 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael G. Balmages 
Chair, Orange County 
Democratic Party 
828 N. Broadway 
Santa Ana, CA 
92701 
Dear Mr. Balmages: 
March 6, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-145 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on March 3, 1989. 
Your letter has 
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact them 
directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Qc~~ 466{Lo 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Are committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures, and committees formed to do voter registration, subject to the contribution limitations contained in Sections 85300-85307 of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: Committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures, and committees formed to do voter registration are not subject to the contribution limitations as long as the committees do not make contributions to candidates. This is because Sections 85300-85307 apply only to candidates and their controlled committees and to committees that make contributions to candidates.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether committees formed for recall measures and voter registration activities are subject to campaign contribution limitations under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding two committees: the Orange County Voters Rights Committee and The Committee to Recall Curt Pringle.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that recall committees and voter registration committees are exempt from contribution limitations under Sections 85300-85307, provided they do not make contributions to candidates. The contribution limitations apply only to candidates, their controlled committees, and committees that contribute to candidates.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes multiple letters: the incoming request from Balmages (Feb 28, 1989), an acknowledgment letter from FPPC (March 6, 1989), and the final confirming advice letter from FPPC (March 20, 1989). Primary FPPC response is the March 20 letter. All sections clearly identified.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures, and committees formed to do voter registration, subject to the contribution limitations contained in Sections 85300-85307 of the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: Committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures, and committees formed to do voter registration are not subject to the contribution limitations as long as the committees do not make contributions to candidates. This is because Sections 85300-85307 apply only to candidates and their controlled committees and to committees that make contributions to candidates.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed telephone advice provided to Michael G. Balmages regarding contribution limitations for committees formed to support recall measures or conduct voter registration activities, clarifying that such committees are not subject to contribution limitations if they do not contribute to candidates.
